title: They Ask You Answer vs Inbound Marketing: Head to Head Comparison
metaDescription: Marcus Sheridan's They Ask You Answer vs Halligan & Shah's Inbound Marketing. Which content marketing framework drives more sales?

book1.title: They Ask You Answer
book1.subtitle: A Revolutionary Approach to Inbound Sales, Content Marketing, and Today's Digital Consumer
book1.author: Marcus Sheridan
book1.description: They Ask, You Answer is a straightforward guide filled with practical tactics and insights for transforming your marketing strategy. This inbound and content marketing book is written by one of the most exciting leaders in sales and marketing, Marcus Sheridan.

book2.title: Inbound Marketing
book2.subtitle: Attract, Engage, and Delight Customers Online
book2.author: Brian Halligan & Dharmesh Shah
book2.description: Get the latest information on inbound marketing to transform your business. Written by HubSpot's founders, this book shows you how to get found by customers online through SEO, blogging, and social media.

book1Strengths: The Big 5 (pricing, problems, comparisons, reviews, best) is instantly actionable
Real case study-Sheridan saved his pool company with these exact tactics
Radical transparency on pricing and problems builds trust immediately
No fluff-every chapter gives you something to implement today
Perfect for B2B and considered purchases (cars, pools, software)
book1Weaknesses: Can feel repetitive-the Big 5 concept gets hammered home a lot
Less strategic depth than Inbound Marketing's broader framework
Sheridan's pool company examples won't resonate with every industry
book2Strengths: Created the entire inbound marketing category-foundational text
HubSpot's research backs up every claim with data
Broader strategic view beyond just content (SEO, social, automation)
The attract-engage-delight framework covers full customer lifecycle
book2Weaknesses: Feels like a long HubSpot sales pitch at times
Less immediately actionable than They Ask You Answer
2014 edition shows age-social media landscape has shifted
More philosophical than tactical for small businesses

book1: If you're not willing to address pricing and cost on your website, you're essentially saying, 'I don't trust my prospects enough to be honest with them.'
Buyers have changed. Your sales and marketing must change too.
The Big 5: Pricing, Problems, Comparisons, Reviews, and Best. Answer these, and you win.
Be the best teacher in your space, and you'll be the best seller.
Assignment selling: send content before meetings to pre-educate buyers.
book2: Get found. Get liked. Get trusted. Get customers.
Inbound marketing is about creating valuable experiences that have a positive impact on people and your business.
People want to do business with other people, not with logos.

book1: You sell something complex or expensive (B2B, services, high-ticket)
You want specific tactics you can implement this week
Your sales team wastes time answering the same questions repeatedly
You're scared to publish pricing or address product weaknesses
You need proof that radical transparency works
book2: You want to understand the inbound marketing philosophy deeply
You're building a comprehensive digital marketing strategy
You need the foundational text that started the movement
You want research and data backing every recommendation
You're considering HubSpot or similar platforms

verdict.summary: They Ask You Answer wins for most businesses-4.7 vs 4.4 stars, and way more actionable. Sheridan gives you the Big 5 topics (pricing, problems, comparisons, reviews, best) that answer what buyers actually search for. It's immediately implementable. Inbound Marketing is the foundational text that created the category, but it's more philosophical and feels like HubSpot marketing. Read They Ask You Answer if you want tactics today. Read Inbound Marketing if you want historical context and broader strategy.
verdict.recommendation: Start with They Ask You Answer-256 pages of pure tactics you can use immediately. Sheridan's pool company case study proves radical transparency works: publishing prices and problems builds trust faster than hiding them. The Big 5 framework tells you exactly what content to create. Implement assignment selling (send articles before sales calls to pre-educate buyers). Then read Inbound Marketing if you want the strategic foundation and are considering marketing automation platforms. Sheridan gives you the what and how; Halligan and Shah give you the why and philosophy. For driving sales now, Sheridan wins.
